mentions.note: Stock beaten down by previous CEO's poor performance; new leadership showing green shoots. Three insider buyers (CEO, former Intel CEO board member, Tim Cook) signal real turnaround. Insiders only buy when bullish.
In continual rebuilding; CEO Elliott Hill silent on product quality improvement (caller concern); turnaround takes 1-2 years minimum; charitable trust owns it; expect improvement with time.

Cramer growing impatient but not giving up; huge turnaround story; expects $70 from current levels; admitting some calls lately have been unbelievable, others not; not selling, has discussion planned for Friday.
No line of sight to return to greatness; China intractable, competition fevered, old inventory lingering. Needs new innovation and US green shoots. Hurting charitable trust.
Cramer's other worst performer; apparel/footwear weakness; brands affiliated with Nike/Home Depot screaming for help.

Caller Blake question: Nike at decade-low prices with attractive dividend. Cramer: Own it for travel trust but it's been a big mistake. Elliott Hill turnaround not happening fast enough. Down 32%, not done yet. No signs of improvement yet.
Wrong call on stock; CEO Elliott Hill reassembling old band when market needs innovation; Piper downgrade on lack of newness justified; give until October for turnaround.
Turnaround thesis unclear and hard to discern; caller rotating out at loss into Levi Strauss; Cramer noted difficulty identifying turn; proceed with caution.

CEO Elliott Hill attempting turnaround but taking longer than expected; revenue down 3%, digital down 9%, Converse down 35%. Will give management a chance, but visible progress in product freshness and share stabilization required before adding.
Disappointing performance; discussed at club meeting yesterday. Giving company one more quarter for redemption; if they disappoint again, have to sell despite long hold period and sunk capital.
